name: Factory Method
description: Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses decide which class to instantiate.
problem: A class can't anticipate the class of objects it must create, or wants to delegate responsibility to subclasses.
solution: Define a factory method that subclasses can override to create specific product types.
when_to_use: When a class can't anticipate the type of objects it needs to create
When a class wants subclasses to specify the objects it creates
When you want to localize the knowledge of which class gets created
when_not_to_use: When object creation is simple and doesn't vary
When there's only one type of object to create

participants.name: Product
participants.role: Defines the interface of objects the factory method creates

participants.name: Creator
participants.role: Declares the factory method which returns a Product object

participants.name: ConcreteCreator
participants.role: Overrides factory method to return a ConcreteProduct instance

pros: Eliminates need to bind application-specific classes
cons: May require creating a subclass just to create a product
